article_meta.title: A California judge scolded members of Mark Zuckerberg's legal team after they showed up to a Los Angeles courtroom weari

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several defensible bias instances in the author's own prose. While the article is relatively straightforward reporting, there are notable instances of loaded language and framing choices by the author that should have been flagged.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged at all, so there are no false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Several defensible bias instances were missed: (1) 'high-stakes trial' - Subjective Qualifying Adjective / Sensationalism, the author characterizes the trial with loaded language; (2) 'claims that strike at the heart of the national debate over Big Tech's influence on America's youth' - Sensationalism/Emotionalism and Spin, the author uses dramatic framing language in their own voice; (3) 'extraordinary misstep' is from a quoted source (Ward) so correctly excluded, but the overall framing and headline choice of 'scolded' is a Spin/Word Choice issue - a loaded interpretive verb used by the author; (4) 'closely watched trial' - Subjective Qualifying Adjective. While some of these are debatable, at least 2-3 are defensible bias instances that were missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied since the array was empty, so no incorrect labels were used.
